zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Lytton於1434年出生在英國德比夏爾柯克 易雷頓時，她的父親Richard Lytton32歲，母親Agnes Hotoft14歲。她和Richard de Blackwell至少有4個兒子和5個女兒。她去世於大約1522年在英國英格蘭德比夏爾布萊克韋爾享年89歲，埋葬在英國德比夏爾布萊克韋爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lytton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Litton
meanings: English: habitational name from Litton (Derbyshire, Dorset, Somerset and Yorkshire), Leyton (Essex), or perhaps Liddaton Green in Bentor or Leddon in Welcombe (both Devon). The Derbyshire and Yorkshire placenames probably derive from Old English hlith ‘slope’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Devon, Dorset, and Somerset places take their names from rivers named with Old English hlȳde ‘the loud one, noisy stream’ + Old English tūn. The Essex place takes its name from the river Lea (of uncertain origin) + Old English tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Lytton was born in 1434, in Kirk Ireton, Derbyshire, England, her father, Richard Lytton, was 32 and her mother, Agnes Hotoft, was 14. She had at least 4 sons and 5 daughters with Richard de Blackwell. She died about 1522, in Blackwell, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 89, and was buried in Blackwell, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Lytton est née en 1434, à Kirk Ireton, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Lytton, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Agnes Hotoft, avait 14 ans. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles avec Richard de Blackwell. Elle est décédée environ 1522, à Blackwell, Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 89 ans, et a été enterrée à Blackwell, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Lytton 1434 in Kirk Ireton, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Lytton 32 und ihre Mutter Agnes Hotoft 14. Sie hatte mit Richard de Blackwell mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb um 1522 im Alter von 89 Jahren in Blackwell, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Blackwell, Derbyshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Lyttonが1434年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアキルク アイレトンで生まれた時，父Richard Lyttonは32歳，母Agnes Hotoftは14歳であった。彼女はRichard de Blackwellとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘をもうけた。彼女は約1522年にイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアブラックウエルで89歳で死去し，イギリスデルブイシュアイアブラックウエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Lytton(이)가 1434년 영국 더비실르 커크 이레턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Lytton(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Hotoft(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 Richard de Blackwell(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1522년 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 블랙웰에서 89세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 더비실르 블랙웰에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Lytton родилась в 1434 г. в местности кирк иретон, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Lytton было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes Hotoft, 14. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей от Richard de Blackwell. Она умерла около 1522 г. в местности блэкквелл, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 89 лет и была похоронена в местности блэкквелл, дирбишир, Англия.
